steps.title: Solve the quadratic
steps.content: Factor the equation: $$x^2-12x-13=0$$ $$(x-13)(x+1)=0$$ So the two solutions are $x=13$ and $x=-1$.

steps.title: Match the keyed form
steps.content: The keyed answer uses the solution $x=13$ and writes it in the requested form: $$13=15-\frac{1}{2}\sqrt{16}$$ Thus one possible match is $h=15$ and $k=16$.

steps.title: Note the source ambiguity
steps.content: The prompt says only that $h$ and $k$ are integer constants, so the representation is not unique without an extra restriction. Choice C matches the keyed representation above, but the current source text does not uniquely determine $h+k$.
